engineCodes.h2: Seat Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Seat engine — EA211 petrol TSI, EA288 diesel TDI, and EA888 turbo families. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Seat specialists.

groups.name: EA211 Petrol Family (2012‑Present) — 1.0‑1.5L TSI/MPI
groups.failureNote: EA211 TSI engines are known for timing chain tensioner wear and direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves. Our rebuilt units include upgraded tensioners, decarbonised ports, and refreshed PCV systems to prevent repeat failures.

groups.name: EA288 Diesel Family (2012‑Present) — 1.4‑2.0L TDI
groups.failureNote: EA288 TDI engines commonly suffer DPF regeneration failure and EGR cooler blockages leading to MOT failures. Our rebuilt units include cleaned/replaced DPF cores, updated EGR valves, and AdBlue system checks for compliance-ready replacements.

groups.name: EA888 Turbo Petrol Family (2008‑Present) — 1.8‑2.0L TFSI
groups.failureNote: EA888 TFSI engines are prone to timing chain tensioner failure and oil pump drive wear causing catastrophic lubrication loss. Our rebuilt units include Gen‑3 tensioners, upgraded oil pumps, and refreshed turbochargers for long‑term reliability.

groups.name: Legacy TDI Engines (Pre‑2012) — 1.9‑2.0L Pump‑Duse/Common Rail
groups.failureNote: Legacy TDI engines often experience injector pump wear and early DPF clogging on high‑mileage units. Our rebuilt units include refurbished injection systems, cleaned turbochargers, and MOT‑ready emissions components

commonProblems.h2: Common Seat Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure – Why Seat EA211 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Seat Ibiza, Leon, Toledo, Arona (2010–2018, engine codes CBZA, CBZB, CHZB, CAXA, CPTA)
problems.rootCause: Early EA211 timing chain tensioners lacked sufficient hydraulic damping, allowing chain stretch under load. Combined with extended oil-change intervals, this accelerates wear on guides and sprockets, leading to rattling, misfires, or catastrophic interference failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, and seals; reset adaptation values via VCDS/OBD | Viable only if caught before rattling or misfire occurs; prevents catastrophic failure but doesn't address underlying design weakness

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Engine‑Out Chain Repair with Valve Inspection
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove engine, replace full timing kit, inspect valves/pistons for contact damage, replace water pump and ancillaries while accessible | Appropriate if chain has stretched but no valve contact; adds labour cost but avoids full rebuild if internals are undamaged

| Supply & fit of bench-tested rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, reinforced chain, and revised oil pump; includes 12-month warranty | Best value when valve contact has occurred or engine has high mileage; upgraded components address root cause and extend service life

problems.recommendation: On a Seat Ibiza or Leon worth £3,000–£6,000, preventative chain replacement at 60,000 miles offers strong ROI if the engine is otherwise healthy. However, once rattling, misfires, or loss of compression appear, the risk of bent valves makes full replacement the safer, more cost-effective route. We recommend comparing rebuilt EA211 engine quotes before authorising major repairs.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt CBZA/CBZB engine prices for your Seat model

problems.h4: High Oil Consumption – The 1.2 TSI Piston Ring Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Seat Ibiza, Leon, Toledo, Mii (2010–2017, engine codes CBZA, CBZB, CHZB, CJZA)
problems.rootCause: Early EA211 1.2 TSI pistons used ring designs prone to carbon coking and loss of tension, allowing oil to bypass into the combustion chamber. This is exacerbated by short-trip driving and extended service intervals.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace PCV valve, clean breather hoses, perform compression test to rule out ring failure | Only suitable if oil consumption is mild (<1L/1,000 miles) and compression is healthy; temporary mitigation, not a fix

| Remove cylinder head, replace piston rings with updated design, hone bores, replace gaskets and seals | Viable if block is undamaged and mileage is under 80,000; labour-intensive and carries risk of incomplete resolution if bore wear exists

| Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded piston rings, revised oil control design, and bench dyno validation; 12-month warranty included | Most reliable solution for engines over 80,000 miles or with confirmed bore scoring; eliminates recurrence risk and restores oil consumption to spec

problems.recommendation: If your 1.2 TSI is consuming over 1 litre of oil per 1,000 miles and has exceeded 70,000 miles, piston ring replacement rarely offers lasting value. We recommend comparing rebuilt engine quotes — the price difference versus a major ring job is often marginal, but the warranty and reliability uplift is significant.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt 1.2 TSI engines with upgraded piston rings

problems.h4: TDI DPF & EGR Failure – Emissions System Blockages Driving Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Seat Leon, Ateca, Alhambra, Toledo (2010–2020, engine codes CAYC, CLCA, DBKA, CRVA, CRMB)
problems.rootCause: Short-trip urban driving prevents DPF regeneration cycles from completing, causing soot accumulation. Simultaneously, EGR valves carbon up from recirculated exhaust gases, restricting flow and triggering fault codes. Combined, these failures increase backpressure, reduce performance, and risk MOT failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Off-vehicle DPF chemical clean or forced regeneration; remove, clean, and test EGR valve; reset adaptation values | Suitable for early-stage blockages with no fault codes; requires regular long-distance driving post-service to prevent recurrence

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – DPF or EGR Component Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace DPF assembly or EGR valve with OEM-spec parts; update ECU software; perform adaptation reset | Viable if only one component has failed and engine health is good; does not address underlying driving-pattern causes

| Supply & fit of rebuilt EA288 engine with DPF-ready configuration, updated EGR cooler, and emissions-system validation; 12-month warranty | Best option when DPF/EGR failures coincide with high mileage, turbo wear, or injector issues; ensures MOT compliance and long-term reliability

problems.recommendation: For Seat TDI owners doing mostly short journeys, component-level repairs often recur within 12–18 months. If your Leon or Ateca has exceeded 90,000 miles and shows multiple emissions faults, a rebuilt DPF-ready engine offers better lifetime value and peace of mind. Always confirm the replacement unit includes updated EGR hardware.
problems.cta: → Compare DPF-ready rebuilt 1.6/2.0 TDI engine prices for your Seat

problems.h4: TSI Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves – Direct Injection Performance Loss
problems.affectedModels: Seat Leon, Ateca, Exeo, Alhambra (2012–2020, engine codes CPTA, CZDA, CXSA, CJZA)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection petrol engines spray fuel directly into the cylinder, bypassing the intake valves. Without fuel washing over the valves, carbon deposits accumulate from crankcase vapours and EGR flow, restricting airflow and causing misfires, rough idle, and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove intake manifold, blast intake valves with crushed walnut media to remove carbon deposits; clean throttle body and MAF sensor | Effective for restoring performance if caught before severe buildup; recommended every 40,000–50,000 miles as preventative maintenance

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Intake Manifold & Valve Service with Gasket Renewal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Full intake removal, carbon cleaning, replace intake gaskets, PCV hoses, and inspect injectors; reset adaptation values | Suitable if carbon has caused rough running or fault codes; adds component renewal to extend service interval post-clean

| Supply & fit of rebuilt EA211/EA888 engine with updated intake design, revised PCV system, and dyno validation; 12-month warranty | Recommended only if carbon buildup has caused valve damage, severe misfires, or coincides with other major failures; overkill for carbon alone

problems.recommendation: Carbon buildup is a maintenance issue, not a terminal failure. For most Seat TSI owners, periodic walnut blasting (£350–£600) at 50,000-mile intervals is far more cost-effective than engine replacement. Only consider a rebuilt engine if carbon has caused physical valve damage or if the engine has additional high-mileage wear factors.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for walnut blast carbon cleaning or rebuilt TSI engines

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure – Boost Loss and Oil Leaks on Seat Petrol & Diesel
problems.affectedModels: Seat Ibiza, Leon, Ateca, Arona (2010–2023, engine codes CHZB, CZPB, CAYC, CLCA, CXSA)
problems.rootCause: Turbochargers rely on consistent oil pressure and temperature control. Extended oil-change intervals, contaminated oil, or clogged oil feed lines cause bearing wear, shaft play, and seal failure. On TDI engines, EGR soot accelerates turbo vane sticking.

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – Turbo Actuator or Wastegate Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace faulty actuator, clean wastegate linkage, reset boost adaptation; test under load | Only viable for electrical actuator faults or stuck wastegates; does not address internal bearing wear

| Remove old turbo, fit refurbished or new OEM-spec unit, replace oil feed/return lines, flush oil system, reset adaptations | Suitable if engine internals are healthy and failure was isolated to turbo; requires high-quality oil and timely servicing post-install

| Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo oiling system, revised breather design, and bench dyno validation; 12-month warranty | Best choice when turbo failure has contaminated the intercooler with oil/debris or coincides with high-mileage wear; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If your Seat turbo has failed but the engine shows good compression and no oil contamination in the intercooler, a quality turbo replacement offers excellent value. However, if oil has entered the intake system or the engine has exceeded 90,000 miles, comparing rebuilt engine quotes is prudent — the marginal cost difference buys comprehensive warranty coverage and upgraded internal components.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt turbo-ready Seat engine prices for your model

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Seat
finalCta.paragraph: Every problem group above shares a common thread: once critical damage occurs, repair costs escalate rapidly while reliability remains uncertain. EngineMarket makes it simple to bypass this uncertainty. Compare prices from trusted UK Seat specialists in seconds, with every rebuilt engine featuring upgraded components that address the original failure point, bench-tested performance validation, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Nationwide delivery and certified supply-and-fit options mean you're back on the road faster — without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Seat Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Seat model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Seat Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Compare all Seat engine types via our UK network, with indicative price ranges matched to your model, mileage and daily driving needs.

types.title: Seat Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected Seat engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the main dealer price.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,400 – £3,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Seat Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete Seat engine rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as reinforced timing chain kits and uprated piston rings to resolve 1.2/1.4 TSI oil burn – for a longer service life than a standard Seat engine swap.

types.title: Used Seat Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage Seat engine removed from a verified donor vehicle. The most affordable Seat engine option when budget is the priority, with documented history and warranty coverage available.

types.title: Seat Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec Seat engine rebuilt to as‑new condition: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit meets exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance variants or long‑term retention.

types.title: Seat Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested Seat engine that delivers reliable performance without undergoing a full rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast Seat engine solution for older or budget‑sensitive models where a complete recon isn’t economical.

types.title: Seat Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement Seat engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, diagnostics and a separate workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £400 – £900 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Seat engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Seat model today.

engineSizes.h2: Seat Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model

items.title: Seat 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L TDI diesel was fitted to earlier-generation Seat hatchbacks and MPVs, offering strong low-end torque for urban driving while meeting Euro 5 emissions standards. Across the brand, these EA288-family units are most commonly replaced due to DPF clogging from short-trip use and EGR valve carbon buildup that triggers fault codes and MOT failure risk
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat 1.4L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration failure on urban cycles
EGR valve sticking from soot accumulation

items.title: Seat 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Seat's 1.6L TDI is the brand's highest-volume diesel displacement, powering Leon, Ibiza, Arona and Ateca models from 2010 onward. Built on the EA288 platform, these engines deliver strong economy but frequently require replacement due to combined DPF/EGR system failures, EGR cooler leaks, and early-model timing chain wear on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat 1.6L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: timing chain stretch on pre-2015 EA288 units

items.title: Seat 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TDI represents Seat's premium diesel offering, fitted to Alhambra, Exeo, Leon, Ateca and Tarraco models. These common-rail EA288 engines are robust but see replacement demand driven by high-pressure fuel pump wear, turbo actuator faults, and compounded DPF/EGR failures that make repair uneconomical beyond 100,000 miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Seat 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Seat's downsized 1.0L TSI three-cylinder turbo petrol powers the Ibiza, Arona and Leon, delivering strong efficiency for urban commuters. These EA211 Evo units are generally reliable but see replacement demand from turbocharger oil-feed coking on neglected services and direct-injection carbon buildup affecting intake valves at higher mileages
items.cta: Compare Seat 1.0L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Turbo oil-feed coking from extended service intervals

items.title: Seat 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L TSI was Seat's volume petrol engine across Ibiza, Leon and Toledo models from 2010–2018. Early EA211 units are notorious for timing chain tensioner failure and high oil consumption from piston ring coking — two failure patterns that frequently drive owners toward rebuilt engine replacement rather than partial repair
items.cta: Compare Seat 1.2L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: high oil consumption from piston ring coking

items.title: Seat 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Seat's 1.4L TSI and MPI engines served Leon, Ibiza, Toledo and Alhambra models across two generations. Direct-injection TSI variants (CAXA, CPTA) commonly require replacement due to severe intake valve carbon buildup restricting airflow, while earlier EA111 units saw timing chain stretch issues that accelerate wear on guides and sprockets
items.cta: Compare Seat 1.4L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: timing chain stretch on early EA111 units

items.title: Seat 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The newer 1.5L TSI Evo replaces the 1.4L across Leon, Arona and Ateca models from 2017 onward, featuring cylinder deactivation for improved economy. As a relatively recent design, catastrophic failure patterns are still emerging, but early replacement demand stems from injector carboning and mild-hybrid electrical system faults that complicate diagnostics
items.cta: Compare Seat 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: Seat's naturally aspirated 1.6L MPI petrol engine provided reliable, low-cost motoring for Ibiza, Toledo and Alhambra models. These CRMB-coded units are among Seat's most dependable, with replacement demand typically driven by age-related coil pack failure or thermostat housing leaks rather than catastrophic internal wear
items.cta: Compare Seat 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L TFSI powered the Exeo and performance Leon FR models, offering strong mid-range torque for enthusiastic driving. These EA888 Gen2 units are most commonly replaced due to oil pump drive chain failure — a known weakness that can cause sudden oil pressure loss and catastrophic bearing damage if not addressed preventatively
items.cta: Compare Seat 1.8L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump drive chain failure (EA888 Gen2)

items.title: Seat 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Seat's 2.0L TSI/TFSI engines power the Leon Cupra, Exeo, Ateca and Tarraco, delivering performance and towing capability. Across EA888 generations, replacement demand is driven by oil pump drive failure on pre-2012 units, timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage examples, and turbocharger bearing fatigue on heavily used performance models
items.cta: Compare Seat 2.0L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: turbo bearing fatigue on high-mileage units

items.title: Seat 1.4L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Seat's plug-in hybrid models combine a 1.4L TSI petrol engine with an electric motor and high-voltage battery, fitted to Leon and Ateca e-Hybrid variants from 2020 onward. Replacement demand remains low but specialist, with battery health degradation and high-voltage system faults requiring certified diagnostic equipment and VW Group-compatible rebuilt hybrid powertrains. System / motor code(s): EA211 TSI PHEV + E-Motor
items.cta: Enquire about Seat 1.4L hybrid engine availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Seat needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Seat specialists.

fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Seat engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply – from the most popular diesel workhorses to the latest electrified powertrains.

items.title: Seat Diesel Engines
items.description: Seat diesel engines cover many of the brand's highest-mileage UK models. Replacement demand is usually driven by timing system wear, injector and turbo faults, plus EGR or DPF-related damage once repeat short-journey use starts affecting reliability.
knownFor: DPF regeneration failure on urban cycles
EGR valve sticking from soot accumulation
DPF back-pressure faults
EGR cooler leakage
importantNotes: Short-journey use increases DPF, EGR, and oil-dilution risk on many diesel engines.
A documented service history matters more on diesel replacements because injector, turbo, and timing faults often build gradually.
items.cta: Compare Seat diesel engine prices

items.title: Seat Petrol Engines
items.description: Seat petrol engines span everything from core daily-driver models to higher-output performance variants. Most replacement enquiries start with timing chain noise, oil consumption, cooling-system failure, or internal wear that makes a warranted replacement more sensible than repeated repairs.
knownFor: Turbo oil-feed coking from extended service intervals
intake valve carbon buildup (direct injection)
Timing chain tensioner failure (EA211 early)
high oil consumption from piston ring coking
importantNotes: Cold-start rattles, overheating history, and oil use are the biggest warning signs before a petrol engine replacement becomes necessary.
Direct-injection petrol engines often need specialist attention for carbon build-up alongside the main mechanical repair.
items.cta: Compare Seat petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Seat has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Seat specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Seat Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Seat engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Seat specialists.

entries.title: CBZA 1.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Fitted across the Ibiza 6J/6F, Leon 5F and Altea 5P between 2010 and 2018, this early 1.2 TSI unit is one of the most frequently replaced codes in the UK market. High oil consumption caused by factory-spec piston ring tension loss is the primary driver for replacement, often compounded by timing chain stretch around 60,000 miles. A quality UK-rebuilt CBZA engine includes uprated piston rings, a revised Gen-2 timing chain tensioner kit, and decarbonised intake ports to eliminate repeat oil burn.
entries.cta: Compare CBZA Seat engine prices and get rebuilt quotes

entries.title: CBZB 1.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Sharing the same EA211 architecture as the CBZA but tuned for higher output (105 HP), this code powers popular Leon, Altea and Ibiza variants from 2010–2018. Catastrophic failure typically stems from premature timing chain guide fragmentation and excessive oil bypassing worn ring lands into the combustion chamber. Rebuilt CBZB units supplied through our network feature reinforced piston rings, metal-reinforced chain guides, and a fully serviced PCV valve to prevent recurrence.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBZB Seat engine replacement

entries.title: CHZB 1.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Powering the modern Ibiza 6F, Arona KJ and Leon KL from 2017 onward, the 1.0 TSI CHZB delivers lightweight efficiency but suffers from plastic water pump/thermostat housing cracks that cause rapid coolant loss. Turbocharger wastegate rattle and high-pressure fuel pump seal leaks also prompt owners to seek full swaps rather than partial repairs. Our network supplies rebuilt CHZB units fitted with reinforced coolant flange assemblies, upgraded turbo wastegate linkages, and revised HPFP seals for long-term reliability.
entries.cta: Find affordable CHZB 1.0 TSI rebuilt engines

entries.title: DTSB 1.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Used extensively in the Leon KL, Ateca KH and Arona KJ between 2019 and 2024, this Euro 6-compliant 1.0 TSI variant experiences high demand for replacement due to internal coolant leaks mixing with engine oil. Carbon accumulation on direct injection valves and early turbo actuator sticking further reduce lifespan on high-mileage commuter vehicles. Each rebuilt DTSB unit undergoes a full decarbonisation process, receives reinforced turbo actuator brackets, and features an upgraded aluminium coolant housing.
entries.cta: Compare DTSB Seat engine replacement costs

entries.title: CAXA 1.4L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Found in the Leon 5F, Exeo 3R and Altea 5P from 2009 to 2018, the CAXA 1.4 TSI is widely sought for replacement due to well-documented timing chain tensioner failure that can lead to valve contact. Oil pump drive module seal degradation frequently compounds the issue, resulting in low oil pressure warnings and potential bearing damage. Quality rebuilt CAXA engines include a Gen-3 hydraulic tensioner, reinforced oil pump drive seals, and thoroughly inspected camshaft lobes to restore factory timing tolerances.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAXA Seat engine rebuild

entries.title: CTHB 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The EA211 EVO 1.5 TSI CTHB code powers modern Ateca, Leon and Arona models from 2018 onward, featuring Active Cylinder Technology (ACT) for improved fuel economy. Replacement demand is primarily driven by ACT solenoid valve sticking causing cylinder deactivation errors, alongside early water pump impeller failures. Rebuilt CTHB units are supplied with revised ACT solenoids, upgraded coolant pump housings, and refreshed timing components to deliver reliable long-term performance.
entries.cta: Compare rebuilt CTHB Seat engine prices

entries.title: DJSA 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine A closely related variant to the CTHB, the DJSA 1.5 TSI is fitted across the Leon KL, Tarraco KN and Ateca KH from 2019 to present. Owners frequently seek replacement due to high-pressure fuel pump wear causing lean misfires, combined with turbocharger bearing degradation on poorly serviced examples. Our rebuilt DJSA specification includes a reinforced HPFP plunger assembly, upgraded turbo oil feed lines, and fully balanced rotating assemblies to prevent repeat power loss.
entries.cta: Find DJSA 1.5 TSI replacement engine quotes

entries.title: CLCA 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Engine Widely used in the Leon 5F, Alhambra 7N and Altea 5P from 2012 onward, the CLCA 1.6 TDI replacement demand stems from EGR cooler blockages and DPF differential pressure sensor faults. Swirl flap actuator wear and early turbo vane sticking further compromise emissions compliance and drivability. Rebuilt CLCA units supplied through our network include a revised EGR cooler matrix, updated DPF pressure sensors, and reinforced turbo actuator linkages to pass MOT requirements reliably.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt CLCA TDI engines near you

entries.title: DBKA 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Engine Sharing the EA288 architecture with the CLCA but tuned for slightly higher output, the DBKA code powers Leon, Ateca and Alhambra models from 2015 onward. Owners frequently request replacement due to timing belt tensioner wear causing belt slippage, compounded by fuel injector tip coking. Each rebuilt DBKA engine features a reinforced tensioner assembly, professionally cleaned or replaced injectors, and a fully serviced high-pressure fuel rail to restore optimal atomisation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for DBKA Seat engine replacement

entries.title: CWZA 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine The flagship CWZA 2.0 TDI code drives the Tarraco KN, Ateca KH and Leon KL from 2017 to present, with high-mileage replacements driven by AdBlue dosing unit blockages and HPFP cam follower wear. Turbocharger actuator failure and EGR valve carbon buildup are additional failure points that trigger full unit swaps. Our rebuilt CWZA specification includes a hardened HPFP cam follower, a revised AdBlue injector nozzle, and a thoroughly cleaned EGR passage for seamless emissions compliance.
entries.cta: Compare CWZA Seat diesel engine prices

entries.title: CFFB 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine Found in the Exeo 3R, Leon FR 5F and Alhambra 7N between 2009 and 2016, the CFFB 170 HP variant suffers from balance shaft bearing wear that causes severe oil pressure drops at high RPM. Turbo actuator sticking and dual-mass flywheel wear frequently compound the damage, making partial repairs uneconomical. Rebuilt CFFB units feature upgraded balance shaft bearings, a heavy-duty turbo wastegate assembly, and reinforced flywheel dowels to eliminate vibration and restore torque delivery.
entries.cta: Get rebuilt CFFB TDI engine quotes

entries.title: CRMB 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine A naturally aspirated workhorse fitted to the Ibiza 6J, Toledo 5P and Alhambra 7N from 2008 to 2015, the CRMB MPI engine is typically replaced following coolant leaks that cause timing belt failure and valve damage. Ignition coil pack degradation and oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals also drive full swaps. Rebuilt CRMB engines include reinforced water pump seals, upgraded ignition coil packs, and a fully flushed lubrication system to prevent repeat overheating.
entries.cta: Compare CRMB naturally aspirated engine costs

entries.title: BXE 1.9L Diesel
entries.description: Engine The legendary BXE 1.9 PD TDI powered the Ibiza 6L, Cordoba 6L and early Altea 5P from 2005 to 2010, but replacement demand remains high due to connecting rod bolt fatigue on modified examples. Oil pump drive failure and injector copper seat leakage are common secondary issues that force owners to seek full replacement. Rebuilt BXE units are supplied with upgraded ARP-style conrod bolts, reinforced injector seats, and a revised oil pump drive assembly for dependable high-mileage use.
entries.cta: Get quotes for BXE 1.9 TDI replacement

entries.title: BKC 1.9L Diesel
entries.description: Engine Sharing the same 1.9 PD TDI architecture as the BXE, the BKC code is found in Altea, Toledo and early Leon models from 2003 to 2009, frequently replaced due to dual-mass flywheel failure transferring vibration into the crankshaft. Turbocharger actuator wear and EGR valve sticking further degrade performance on poorly maintained units. Rebuilt BKC engines include a reinforced flywheel mounting surface, a heavy-duty turbo actuator, and a professionally cleaned EGR system to restore smooth idle and acceleration.
entries.cta: Find BKC PD TDI rebuilt engine deals

entries.title: AZV 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine An early 2.0 TDI unit used in Altea 5P, Toledo 5P and Leon 1P between 2004 and 2009, the AZV code requires replacement primarily due to oil pump balance shaft bearing wear that causes rapid oil pressure loss. Head gasket failure between adjacent cylinders and injector seat degradation are common compounding factors on high-mileage vehicles. Quality rebuilt AZV engines feature revised balance shaft bearings, multi-layer steel head gasket kits, and reinforced injector seats to eliminate oil starvation and coolant leaks.
entries.cta: Compare AZV Seat diesel engine prices

entries.title: BMM 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine Fitted to Altea, Toledo and Leon variants from 2005 to 2010, the BMM 140 HP PD TDI suffers from EGR valve sticking and turbo wastegate failure that trigger limp mode and power loss. Oil sludge buildup from neglected cam cover breather hoses often accelerates turbo bearing wear. Rebuilt BMM units include a heavy-duty EGR valve assembly, a reinforced turbo wastegate actuator, and a fully decarbonised intake manifold to ensure reliable daily driving performance.
entries.cta: Get quotes for BMM Seat engine replacement

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Seat engine code directory below — every code from the last 20 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.h3: Full Seat Engine Code Directory (All Years)
directory.label: “View all Seat engine codes (60+ codes, 1990–2025)”
directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every Seat engine code from the last three decades. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: SEAT Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: VW Group Integration: First-Generation TDI & 1.8T Petrol Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The launch of the SEAT Leon and Ibiza Mk3 in 1999–2000 marked SEAT's full integration into the VW Group engine architecture - bringing the proven 1.8T and TDI families from the Audi/VW platform into the Spanish brand for the first time and establishing the engine commonality that defines SEAT to this day.
keyChanges: 1.8T (AGU, AUM, BAM) introduced across Leon Mk1 (1M) and Ibiza 6K - shared directly with Golf Mk4/Audi A3 petrol family
1.9 TDI pump-duse (AFN, AHF, AGR, AXR) introduced across Leon and Toledo - common-rail and distributor-pump variants available
Leon Cupra R (1M) launched 2002 with 1.8T BAM 225hp - first high-performance SEAT in the UK market
2.8 VR6 (AUE) on Leon Cupra 4 - all-wheel drive flagship, 204hp naturally aspirated V6
1.4 AHW and 1.6 AEH/AKL petrols continue across Ibiza and Cordoba as volume entry options
mainEngines: AGU / AUM / BAM - 1.8T turbo petrol 150–225hp (SEAT Leon, Toledo, Ibiza Cupra)
AFN / AHF / AGR - 1.9 TDI diesel 90–110hp (SEAT Leon, Toledo, Alhambra)
AXR / ATD - 1.9 TDI 100hp (SEAT Ibiza 6K/6L, Leon)
AUE - 2.8 VR6 24v petrol 204hp (SEAT Leon Cupra 4)
AHW / AKQ - 1.4 16v petrol 75hp (SEAT Ibiza 6K/6L, Cordoba)
knownFor: 1.8T AGU and BAM are among the most tuneable engines in the VW Group family - deeply supported by the UK aftermarket with a strong enthusiast and replacement specialist network

1.9 TDI AFN and AGR with distributor injection are regarded by UK diesel specialists as among the most bulletproof diesels of the era - simple mechanical architecture and very high-mileage survivability
Leon Cupra R BAM 225hp is a genuine performance landmark for the SEAT brand in the UK - cam follower wear on the injection pump drive is a well-known inspection point at high mileage
VR6 Leon Cupra 4 is a collector's piece now - naturally aspirated and smooth but timing chain maintenance and cooling system reliability must be verified on any used example
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: SEAT Leon 1.8T engine replacement UK - SEAT Leon Cupra R BAM engine for sale - SEAT 1.9 TDI AFN engine reconditioned - SEAT Toledo 1.9 TDI engine replacement
years.cta: Compare 1999–2002 SEAT Leon and Ibiza 1.8T and TDI replacement engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: Ibiza Mk3 PD Diesel & Leon FR Petrol Generation
years.description: The full rollout of pump-duse (PD) direct-injection diesels across the Ibiza and Leon from 2002 gave SEAT the highest-efficiency diesel line-up in its segment, while the Leon FR introduced the 1.8T in a sporty package that quickly became one of the most popular used SEAT buys in the UK.
keyChanges: 1.9 TDI PD (BJB, BKC, BLS) replaces distributor-pump units across Ibiza Mk3 (6L) and Leon Mk1 facelift - higher injection pressure, better fuel economy
1.4 16v TDI (AMF, BHC) introduced on Ibiza 6L - ultra-economy three-cylinder diesel, unique in the segment
Leon FR 1.8T and later Cupra R continues with BAM / AMK - performance focus of the range
1.4 16v petrol (AUA, BBY) updated on Ibiza and Cordoba as volume entry option
Ibiza Cupra Sport (2003) with 1.8T 180hp - performance hot hatch entry point for SEAT in the UK
mainEngines: BJB / BKC / BLS - 1.9 TDI PD diesel 100–130hp (SEAT Ibiza 6L, Leon 1M facelift)
AMF / BHC - 1.4 TDI three-cylinder diesel 70–75hp (SEAT Ibiza 6L)
BAM / AMK - 1.8T turbo petrol 150–180hp (SEAT Leon FR, Ibiza Cupra Sport)
AUA / BBY - 1.4 16v petrol 75–85hp (SEAT Ibiza 6L, Cordoba)
ARL / BTB - 1.9 TDI 150hp (SEAT Alhambra FR diesel, Leon Sport TDI)
knownFor: BKC 1.9 TDI PD is one of the most common SEAT engine replacement searches in the UK - EGR valve failure, swirl flap issues, and injector seal leaks at 80,000–120,000 miles are the defining workshop complaints

1.4 TDI three-cylinder AMF is an unusually frugal engine for its era - fragile timing belt (not chain) requires strict replacement intervals and is the primary cause of catastrophic failures on neglected examples
Ibiza Cupra Sport with 1.8T builds on an already strong enthusiast following - ball bearing turbocharger upgrades and inlet manifold improvements are widely available in the UK
ARL 1.9 TDI 150hp in Alhambra is a strong torque unit but dual-mass flywheel wear between 80,000–100,000 miles is a near-universal complaint across UK examples
years.cta: Find 2002–2005 SEAT Ibiza and Leon TDI PD and 1.8T replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: Leon Mk2 (1P), New Ibiza 6L Late & 2.0 TDI PD Launch Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The second-generation SEAT Leon (1P) launched in 2005 as one of the VW Group's most sporting mid-size hatches - bringing the 2.0 TDI PD (BKD) and the 2.0 TFSI (AXX/BWA) performance engines to SEAT owners for the first time, alongside the brand's most advanced driver-focused lineup to date.
keyChanges: Leon Mk2 (1P) launched 2005 - 2.0 TDI PD (BKD) and 1.9 TDI (BJB, BXE) diesels across FR and S trims
2.0 TFSI (AXX, BWA) introduced on Leon FR and Cupra - replaces 1.8T as the performance petrol benchmark
2.0 TDI 170hp (BMN, BUY) in Leon FR TDI - highest-output diesel SEAT had offered in the UK market
Leon Cupra 2.0 TFSI (BYD) 241hp - SEAT's first truly class-competitive hot hatch in the modern era
New Ibiza Mk4 (6J) development underway - outgoing 6L continues with existing 1.4/1.9 TDI/1.8T codes
mainEngines: BKD / BXE / BJB - 2.0/1.9 TDI PD diesel 105–140hp (SEAT Leon 1P, Altea)
BMN / BUY - 2.0 TDI PD 170hp diesel (SEAT Leon FR TDI)
AXX / BWA - 2.0 TFSI EA113 turbo petrol 200hp (SEAT Leon FR, Cupra)
BYD - 2.0 TFSI EA113 241hp (SEAT Leon Cupra)
BXW / BSE - 1.6 petrol 102hp (SEAT Leon S entry, Altea)
knownFor: BKD 2.0 TDI PD is the single highest-volume SEAT diesel replacement engine in the UK - EGR failure, DPF blockage, and injector seal leaks are universally recognised by independent specialists
2.0 TFSI AXX in early Leon FR and Cupra suffers from carbon deposit build-up on intake valves - walnut blasting is an essential maintenance step at 60,000+ miles on direct-injection examples
Leon Cupra with BYD 241hp is well-regarded for straight-line pace but dual-mass flywheel and clutch wear under spirited use generates frequent replacement enquiries on UK examples
2.0 TDI 170hp BMN requires timing belt replacement at strict intervals - belt failure at high mileage causes catastrophic engine destruction and drives significant reconditioned unit demand
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: SEAT Leon 2.0 TDI BKD engine replacement - SEAT Leon FR 2.0 TFSI engine for sale UK - SEAT Leon Cupra engine reconditioned - SEAT Altea 1.9 TDI engine replacement - SEAT Leon FR TDI 170 engine UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005–2008 SEAT Leon Mk2 TDI and TFSI replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: Ibiza Mk4 (6J), EA189 TDI & EA113 TFSI Update
years.description: The Ibiza Mk4 (6J) launched in 2008 as SEAT's highest-volume UK model, bringing revised 1.2 TDI, 1.6 TDI (EA189), and 1.4 TFSI twincharged engines alongside an updated Leon range - establishing the engine family that would later be implicated in the 2015 Dieselgate emissions scandal.
keyChanges: Ibiza Mk4 (6J) launches 2008 - all-new body on PQ25 platform with 1.2 TDI, 1.4 TFSI, and 1.6 TDI engines
1.6 TDI (CAY, CAYB) EA189 engine introduced across Ibiza and Leon from 2009 - replaces 1.9 TDI PD
1.2 TDI (CFWA) - first sub-1.4 diesel in SEAT range, ultra-low CO₂ Ecomotive trim
1.4 TFSI twincharged (CAXC, BLG) continues from Mk1 on Leon - single turbo and supercharger combined
Leon Mk2 facelift (1P LCI) retains 2.0 TDI CBAB/CCHA and 2.0 TFSI CCZB/CDLB for FR/Cupra trim
mainEngines: CAY / CAYB - 1.6 TDI EA189 diesel 75–105hp (SEAT Ibiza 6J, Leon 1P, Altea)
CFWA - 1.2 TDI EA189 three-cylinder diesel 75hp (SEAT Ibiza Ecomotive)
CAXC / BLG - 1.4 TFSI twincharged petrol 150hp (SEAT Leon FR, Ibiza Cupra)
CBAB / CCHA - 2.0 TDI diesel 140–170hp (SEAT Leon FR TDI facelift, Altea)
CCZB / CDLB - 2.0 TFSI EA113 200–211hp (SEAT Leon FR/Cupra facelift)
knownFor: 1.6 TDI EA189 (CAY) becomes the most affected SEAT engine by the 2015 Dieselgate recall - software-fix-only approach maintained unease among UK independent specialists about long-term DPF performance
1.4 TFSI twincharged CAXC is technically sophisticated for its displacement - supercharger-to-turbo handover valve failures and actuator wear are the dominant independent workshop complaint
CBAB 2.0 TDI continues to generate strong replacement demand from Leon FR TDI owners - same fundamental EGR and injector concerns as earlier BKD architecture
Ibiza 6J is one of the most popular UK SEAT replacement engine searches by volume - high fleet and young-driver ownership creates consistent demand from 100,000-mile-plus examples
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: SEAT Ibiza 1.6 TDI engine replacement UK - SEAT Leon 2.0 TDI CBAB engine for sale - SEAT Ibiza 1.4 TFSI engine reconditioned - SEAT Leon Cupra 2.0 TFSI engine replacement - SEAT Ibiza 6J diesel engine UK
years.cta: Compare 2008–2012 SEAT Ibiza and Leon EA189 TDI and TFSI replacement engine prices

years.preview: Leon Mk3 (5F), EA888 Gen 1/2 & EA189 Dieselgate
years.description: The third-generation Leon (5F) launched in 2012 as SEAT's most technically advanced model to date, introducing the EA888 second-generation petrol family and the EA189 TDI that would become infamous three years later in the 2015 Dieselgate emissions scandal affecting hundreds of thousands of UK SEAT owners.
keyChanges: Leon Mk3 (5F) launched 2012 - EA888 Gen 2 (CJZA 1.2 TSI, CHHB/CHPB 1.8 TSI) and EA189 TDI (CLHA 1.6 TDI, CLJA 2.0 TDI)
EA888 Gen 2 CNHA 2.0 TSI 265hp in Leon Cupra - first SEAT Cupra with the EA888 family
EA189 scandal breaks September 2015 - 1.6 TDI (CLHA, CAY) and 2.0 TDI (CLJA) SEAT models in UK recall
Ibiza Mk4 facelift (6J LCI) 2012 - 1.2 TSI (CBZB, CJZA) replaces ageing 1.4 TFSI as volume petrol unit
Leon X-Perience (5F all-road) introduced - 2.0 TDI 150hp with four-wheel-drive drivetrain
mainEngines: CJZA / CBZB - 1.2 TSI EA111 petrol 85–105hp (SEAT Ibiza 6J facelift, Leon 5F entry)
CHHB / CHPB - 1.8 TSI EA888 Gen 2 petrol 180hp (SEAT Leon FR 5F)
CNHA / CJXB - 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 2 petrol 265hp (SEAT Leon Cupra 5F)
CLHA / CAY - 1.6 TDI EA189 diesel 90–110hp (SEAT Ibiza, Leon 5F)
CLJA / CLLB - 2.0 TDI EA189 diesel 150–184hp (SEAT Leon FR TDI, X-Perience)
knownFor: EA888 Gen 2 CNHA Cupra is broadly well-regarded - water pump failure, thermostat housing leaks, and timing chain stretch on early high-mileage examples are the dominant UK specialist complaints

EA189 Dieselgate recall is the defining quality event of SEAT's modern history in the UK - software update resolved emissions test behaviour but long-term DPF performance post-recall remains a concern for independent diesel specialists

1.2 TSI CJZA earns consistent praise for economy and smoothness in the Ibiza - PCV valve failure causing oil consumption and timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileages are the key independent workshop concerns

Leon Mk3 ST estate with 2.0 TDI generates the highest combined family-buyer replacement engine demand for SEAT in the UK from 2015 onwards - TDI estate replacement is the most-searched Leon engine enquiry category
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: SEAT Leon Cupra 2.0 TSI engine replacement UK - SEAT Leon FR 1.8 TSI engine for sale - SEAT Ibiza 1.2 TSI engine reconditioned - SEAT Leon 2.0 TDI EA189 replacement - SEAT Leon Mk3 diesel engine UK
years.cta: Find 2012–2015 SEAT Leon Mk3 EA888 and EA189 replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: EA888 Gen 3 Cupra 290, 1.5 TSI Evo & Post-Dieselgate EA288 Major Reliability Revision
years.description: SEAT's post-Dieselgate period saw the EA288 TDI replace EA189 diesels across the Leon and Ibiza, while the EA888 Gen 3 pushed the Cupra to 290hp - the highest-output SEAT UK production car at the time - and the new 1.5 TSI Evo arrived as the brand's most efficient petrol in 2017.
keyChanges: EA888 Gen 3 (DJHA, CZPB, CJXC) replaces Gen 2 in Leon FR and Cupra - improved chain guide, dual injection, raised output ceiling to 290hp
Leon Cupra 290 and 300 arrive - DJHA 2.0 TSI 290hp with performance pack available, DSG or manual
EA288 1.6 TDI (DGTE, DGDB) and 2.0 TDI (DFCA, DETA) begin replacing EA189 units from 2015–2016
1.0 TSI (CHZB, DKJA) introduced on Ibiza Mk5 (KJ) 2017 - first three-cylinder turbocharged petrol in mainstream SEAT range
1.5 TSI Evo (DADA, DPCA) introduced 2017 on Leon - Euro 6d petrol with cylinder deactivation (ACT), strong efficiency claims
mainEngines: DJHA / CZPB - 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 3 petrol 290–300hp (SEAT Leon Cupra 5F facelift)
CJXC / CHHA - 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 3 200hp (SEAT Leon FR 5F facelift)
DADA / DPCA - 1.5 TSI Evo petrol 130hp (SEAT Leon 5F late / Arona)
DGTE / DGDB - 1.6 TDI EA288 diesel 90–115hp (SEAT Ibiza, Leon 5F)
DFCA / DETA - 2.0 TDI EA288 diesel 150hp (SEAT Leon FR TDI 5F facelift)
CHZB / DKJA - 1.0 TSI three-cylinder petrol 95–115hp (SEAT Ibiza Mk5, Arona)
knownFor: EA888 Gen 3 in Cupra 290 is regarded as the most capable SEAT performance engine of the combustion era - strong aftermarket, 350hp+ tune potential on standard internals, and broadly reliable at standard output with quality oil

1.5 TSI Evo cold-start hesitation and kangarooing on manual gearbox cars is a widely documented UK fault - a software patch was released in February 2020 but some owners continue to report residual cold-running roughness

EA288 1.6 TDI is a meaningful improvement over EA189 - fewer injector seal failures and better real-world DPF performance, though AdBlue system complexity adds a new maintenance cost from this generation

1.0 TSI three-cylinder is broadly praised for refinement and economy in the Ibiza and Arona - oil consumption on high-mileage examples and PCV breather system blockage are the most frequent UK independent workshop findings
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: SEAT Leon Cupra 290 engine replacement UK - SEAT Leon FR 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 3 for sale - SEAT Ibiza 1.0 TSI engine reconditioned - SEAT Leon 1.5 TSI engine replacement - SEAT Leon 2.0 TDI EA288 engine UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015–2018 SEAT Leon Cupra and FR EA888 Gen 3 replacement engines

years.preview: Cupra Brand Split, Leon Mk4, 48V e-Hybrid & EA888 Gen 4 Technology Introduction Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: SEAT's most transformational period since VW Group integration - Cupra became an independent brand in 2018, the Leon Mk4 (KL) launched in 2020 on the MQB Evo platform with EA888 Gen 4 and 48V mild hybrid, and the e-Hybrid plug-in powertrain arrived as the brand's first electrified production offering for the UK.
keyChanges: Cupra brand launched as independent entity from SEAT in 2018 - Cupra Ateca and Cupra Leon carry forward from SEAT Leon Cupra engineering base
Leon Mk4 (KL) launched 2020 - EA888 Gen 4 (DKZA 2.0 TSI) replaces Gen 3 across FR and Cupra variants
1.5 TSI Evo (DPCA, DADA) continues with post-2020 software fix on Leon Mk4 and Arona
Leon e-Hybrid (PHEV) introduced - 1.4 TSI (DGEA) combined with 13kWh electric motor, 204hp system
Cupra Formentor (2021) launches with 2.0 TSI (DNFA, DNU) 190–310hp - first unique Cupra platform model
mainEngines: DKZA / DLBA - 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 4 petrol 190–300hp (Cupra Leon, SEAT Leon FR Mk4)
DNFA / DNUA - 2.0 TSI petrol 190–310hp (Cupra Formentor, Cupra Ateca)
DPCA / DADA - 1.5 TSI Evo petrol 130–150hp (SEAT Leon Mk4, Arona, Ibiza Mk5 facelift)
DGEA / DDSA - 1.4 TSI e-Hybrid PHEV 204hp (SEAT Leon e-Hybrid, Cupra Leon e-Hybrid)
DGTE / DGDB - 1.6 TDI EA288 diesel (SEAT Ibiza, Arona continued)
DFCA / DCYA - 2.0 TDI EA288 diesel 150hp (SEAT Leon Mk4 FR TDI, Ateca)
knownFor: EA888 Gen 4 in Cupra Leon and Formentor is broadly regarded as the most polished iteration of the engine family - dual injection reduces intake valve carbon build-up substantially versus Gen 3, and thermal management is improved

Leon e-Hybrid PHEV earns strong fleet and company-car appeal in the UK but petrol engine oil dilution from cold short-cycle driving (PHEV running on electric only) is an emerging specialist concern at high mileage
Cupra Formentor with 2.0 TSI DNFA 310hp is the performance highlight of this generation - 400hp+ tune potential on standard internals, with strong UK specialist and aftermarket support

48V mild hybrid is not yet available on SEAT-branded models - the electrification architecture is concentrated in PHEV form; this differentiates SEAT from VW Group siblings that adopt 48V mild hybrid from 2019 onward
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Cupra Leon 2.0 TSI engine replacement UK - SEAT Leon Mk4 FR engine for sale - Cupra Formentor 2.0 TSI engine reconditioned - SEAT Leon e-Hybrid engine replacement - SEAT Arona 1.0 TSI engine UK
years.cta: Compare 2018–2021 SEAT Leon Mk4 and Cupra EA888 Gen 4 replacement engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: Cupra Born Electric, Euro 6d Final TDI & Ibiza/Arona Refresh Technology Introduction
years.description: The 2021 Cupra Born marked SEAT Group's entry into full battery-electric production - the first fully electric Cupra built in Germany on the MEB platform - while combustion Leon, Ibiza, and Arona models were refreshed under Euro 6d regulations, and the 1.0 TSI three-cylinder consolidated its position as the dominant SEAT petrol engine in the UK.
keyChanges: Cupra Born (VZ1) launched 2021 - 231hp fully electric on VW MEB platform, no combustion engine
Ibiza Mk5 and Arona mid-life facelift - 1.0 TSI (DKJA, DLAC) updated for Euro 6d, cylinder deactivation added on higher-output variants
SEAT drops 2.0 TDI from Ibiza and Arona range - diesel confined to Leon and Ateca in the UK fleet
Leon Mk4 facelift (2023) - EA888 Gen 4 continues across FR and Cupra, minor ECU calibration updates for Euro 6e preparation
Cupra Formentor VZ5 (2022) - 2.5 TFSI five-cylinder (DNF) 390hp in limited production; highest-output SEAT Group combustion car sold in the UK
mainEngines: DLAC / DKJA - 1.0 TSI three-cylinder petrol 90–115hp Euro 6d (SEAT Ibiza, Arona)
DPCA / DADA - 1.5 TSI Evo petrol 130–150hp (SEAT Leon, Arona top-spec)
DKZA / DLBA - 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 4 petrol 190–300hp (SEAT Leon FR, Cupra Leon)
DNF - 2.5 TFSI I5 five-cylinder 390hp (Cupra Formentor VZ5)
DFCA / DCYA - 2.0 TDI EA288 diesel 150hp (SEAT Leon FR TDI, Ateca TDI continued)
BEV - MEB platform electric motor 231hp (Cupra Born VZ1)
knownFor: Cupra Born earns consistently strong owner satisfaction scores in the UK - real-world range of 200–250 miles on the 77kWh battery suits most urban and suburban UK commutes; fast-charge infrastructure is the limiting factor for longer routes

Formentor VZ5 five-cylinder DNF engine is a rare and enthusiast-celebrated powertrain - Audi RS3-derived, low production numbers, and exceptional driving character make it a modern collector's piece in the UK

1.0 TSI three-cylinder is now the most registered SEAT engine in the UK by volume - PCV failure, oil consumption at 100,000+ miles, and DSG dry-clutch behaviour are the primary independent specialist concerns

Final EA288 TDI models are generating growing replacement enquiries as Euro 6d-era Leon and Ateca diesels accumulate mileage - AdBlue injector crystallisation and DPF blockage remain the leading failure modes
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: SEAT Leon FR 2.0 TSI 2022 engine replacement - Cupra Formentor 2.0 TSI engine for sale UK - SEAT Ibiza 1.0 TSI reconditioned engine - SEAT Leon TDI 2022 engine replacement - Cupra Leon 300 engine UK
years.cta: Get quotes for current-generation SEAT and Cupra combustion replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: New Cupra Tavascan EV, Leon Mk4 Final & Euro 6e Compliance Technology Introduction
years.description: SEAT Group's current range continues the combustion Leon and Ibiza through Euro 6e compliance updates while Cupra expands its electric platform with the Tavascan (2024) - an all-electric SUV on the MEB platform - marking the most significant expansion of the group's EV footprint in the UK to date alongside the ongoing petrol-dominant used-car replacement market.
keyChanges: Cupra Tavascan (VZ2) launched 2024 - fully electric, MEB Evo platform, up to 340hp dual-motor
Cupra Born updated for 2025 - 77kWh battery with 326hp top-spec e-boost, up to 389 miles WLTP claimed
Leon Mk4 Euro 6e compliance updates to EA888 Gen 4 - revised injection mapping and thermal management for real-world RDE standards
SEAT Ibiza and Arona combustion range (1.0 TSI / 1.5 TSI) confirmed as continuing in production - no imminent end-of-combustion announced for light commercial segment
Diesel removed from new Ibiza and Arona order books in UK - Leon remains the last SEAT model available new with a diesel in Britain
mainEngines: DLAC / DKJA - 1.0 TSI petrol Euro 6e (SEAT Ibiza, Arona current)
DPCA - 1.5 TSI Evo petrol 130–150hp (SEAT Arona, Leon continuing)
DKZA / DLBA - 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 4 Euro 6e (Cupra Leon, SEAT Leon FR current)
DFCA / DCYA - 2.0 TDI EA288 Euro 6e diesel (SEAT Leon TDI, Ateca - continuing)
BEV - MEB Evo dual-motor up to 340hp (Cupra Tavascan)
BEV - MEB 230–326hp (Cupra Born 2025)
knownFor: Cupra Tavascan receives strong UK reception as a premium electric SUV - dual-motor 340hp all-wheel-drive variant is the performance flagship but single-motor base variant commands the largest UK order volume

Updated Cupra Born 326hp e-boost makes it one of the fastest hot hatches available in the UK by 0–62 time - thermal management of e-boost delivery in sustained track use is a reported limitation in owner reviews

Leon combustion range continues to generate the dominant share of SEAT replacement engine demand in the UK - EA888 Gen 4 FR and Cupra units from 2020–2024 entering the high-volume used-car replacement cycle

Removal of diesel from Ibiza and Arona new-car orders increases used-car demand for existing TDI units - independent specialists expect EA288 1.6 TDI replacement demand to remain elevated through 2028
years.cta: Enquire about current-generation SEAT and Cupra combustion and performance engine replacement availability from UK specialists

faq.h2: Seat Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: ** Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Seat owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Seat engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Reconditioned Seat engines typically range from £1,200–£2,800 for common 1.0–1.6L petrol or diesel units, while rebuilt 2.0L TSI/TDI engines with upgraded components cost £1,800–£3,500. Fitting adds £400–£900 depending on model complexity, significantly less than main dealer rates. For budget-conscious owners, used engines start around £400–£1,200 but carry higher risk without warranty coverage. We always recommend comparing at least three verified quotes to secure the best value for your Seat engine replacement cost.
items.cta: Compare Seat engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Seat engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors drive your Seat engine fitting cost: displacement (1.0L vs 2.0L), condition (used vs reconditioned vs rebuilt), vehicle complexity (Ibiza vs Alhambra AWD), and whether you choose supply-only or Seat engine supply and fit service. Location matters too — London and South East labour rates can be 20–30% higher than regional specialists. Our comparison tool provides transparent, postcode-matched quotes so you see exactly what affects your engine swap cost Seat estimate before committing.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Seat engine specialists today

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Seat?
items.answer: In most cases yes — if your Leon, Ibiza or Ateca chassis and gearbox are solid, a rebuilt Seat engine with upgraded timing components or piston rings often outlasts the original unit. With UK used Seat values holding steady, investing £1,500–£3,000 in a warranty-backed replacement is frequently more economical than purchasing a replacement vehicle. Only consider scrapping if structural corrosion or major transmission issues are also present.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Seat engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a Seat engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full Seat engine supply and fit service covers sourcing your exact EA211 or EA288 engine code, professional removal of the failed unit, installation with new gaskets and fluids, post-fit diagnostics, and responsible disposal of your old engine. Most specialists include a workmanship warranty alongside the engine guarantee, with fitting costs typically £400–£900 depending on model access. Always confirm whether VAT, delivery, and ancillary transfer are included in your Seat engine supply and fit cost quote.
items.cta: Compare Seat engine supply and fit deals nationwide

items.question: How long does a Seat engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like the Ibiza or Leon with readily available CBZA, CLCA or CHZB engines, expect 3–5 working days from order to completion. More complex vehicles like the Alhambra or Ateca AWD may require 5–7 days due to additional drivetrain components. The biggest variable is engine availability — our network prioritises high-demand Seat engine codes to minimise downtime.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to check Seat engine stock levels

items.question: Do reconditioned Seat engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every reconditioned Seat engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard, with many rebuilt units offering optional 24-month coverage. This warranty typically covers major internals including the crankshaft, pistons, timing chain, oil pump and cylinder head against manufacturing defects. Always confirm exact Seat engine warranty uk terms directly with your chosen supplier, particularly regarding labour coverage and claim procedures.
items.cta: Find warranty-backed Seat engine replacements now

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Seat engine?
items.answer: Used Seat engines (£400–£1,200) suit temporary fixes or vehicles nearing end-of-life, but carry unknown mileage and minimal warranty protection. Reconditioned units (£1,200–£2,800) include new seals, bearings and often upgraded components addressing known failure points like EA211 timing tensioners or TDI EGR systems — making them the smarter choice for long-term ownership. If your Seat has a failure-prone engine family, investing in a reconditioned unit with comprehensive cover is strongly recommended.
items.cta: Compare used vs recon Seat engine prices today

items.question: Where can I find Seat engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK-wide network of verified Seat engine specialists, with VW Group expertise concentrated in Manchester, Birmingham, London and Glasgow hubs. Our registration lookup instantly matches your chassis number to nearby suppliers who stock your specific EA211, EA288 or EA888 engine code and offer certified fitting. Nationwide palletised delivery is available if no local stock exists, ensuring you find Seat engine specialists near me without compromising on quality or warranty coverage.
items.cta: Get matched with certified Seat engine fitters in your area

items.question: What are the most common Seat engine failures?
items.answer: The most frequent catastrophic issues driving Seat engine replacement include EA211 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner failure (CBZA/CBZB engines), high oil consumption from piston ring coking, direct injection carbon buildup on 1.4/1.5/2.0 TSI intake valves, and EA288 TDI DPF/EGR blockages on urban-commuter Leon and Ibiza models. Early warning signs like rattling, blue smoke or loss of power rarely allow preventative repair — making warranty-backed replacement the most reliable solution for common Seat engine problems.
items.cta: Compare quotes for failure-resistant rebuilt Seat engines

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Seat engine?
items.answer: Comparing multiple quotes is essential — prices for the same CBZA or CLCA engine code can vary by £1,000+ across suppliers. Consider used units for tight budgets, but prioritise reconditioned engines with upgraded components if you plan long-term ownership. Ask whether your quote includes fitting, delivery and VAT, and verify warranty terms before committing. Our free comparison tool lets you evaluate all options side-by-side to find the cheapest way to replace engine uk without sacrificing reliability.
items.cta: Get instant Seat engine price matches from verified suppliers
